jokes.text: One hundred! That's almost ten per year.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth announces their 100th delivery as a milestone achievement
jokes.punchline: Bender's math reveals this is actually terrible productivity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed deflation of Farnsworth's enthusiasm with simple math. Economy and character voice are excellent - only Bender would respond this way.

jokes.text: I'm ordering a hundred kegs, a hundred hookers and a hundred Elvis impersonators who aren't above a little hooking, should the occasion arise.
jokes.setup: Bender wants to celebrate with party supplies
jokes.punchline: Elvis impersonators doubling as hookers creates absurd specificity
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender excess with a creative twist on hookers. The Elvis angle adds originality but runs a bit long.

jokes.text: Mrs. Astor takes nitroglycerine for her heart, and being a class act, she prefers it in soufflé form.
jokes.setup: Elzar explains they're delivering a soufflé
jokes.punchline: The soufflé contains explosive medicine because she's 'classy'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic - taking medicine in fancy form because of 'class.' Sets up the explosive element perfectly.

jokes.text: Bam.. To prevent the soufflé from exploding, Bender will carry it. He's gyroscopically stable.
jokes.setup: Need someone stable to carry explosive soufflé
jokes.punchline: Choosing notoriously unstable Bender because he's 'technically' stable
jokes.explanation: Good character-based irony. Bender being 'gyroscopically stable' while behaviorally chaotic is perfect setup for disaster.

jokes.text: Wow, it's ricketier than it looks. You better try to jump it, Bender.
jokes.punchline: It's even worse than it appears, so naturally send the robot carrying explosives
jokes.explanation: Functional setup joke. The logic of making it worse then suggesting jumping is mildly amusing but predictable.

jokes.text: I shall inform Mrs. Astor that the circus is in town.
jokes.punchline: Butler's understated reaction treating it as circus entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect butler understatement. The formal tone contrasting with chaos is well-executed and economical.

jokes.text: Where's the exploding? One does not explode in Mrs. Astor's face.
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Astor's disappointment and proper etiquette about exploding
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy. Mrs. Astor's proper etiquette about explosions is absurdly perfect and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Yes, I have. And I'm his uncle.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth mentions 200 years in New New York
jokes.punchline: Fry claims to also have 200 years experience and be Farnsworth's uncle
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry stupidity callback to the time travel arc. Perfect character consistency and economy.

jokes.text: How charmingly unconventional. I can eat a hot dog underwater.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Astor politely responds to Fry's bizarre claim
jokes.punchline: Fry follows with completely unrelated underwater hot dog eating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry non-sequitur. Mrs. Astor's politeness makes his random boast even funnier through contrast.

jokes.text: Just four days into her maiden voyage, as we approached 32nd street, the land liner struck a mailbox and went down.
jokes.punchline: Massive vessel destroyed by tiny mailbox after 4 days
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Titanic parody with perfect absurd scale. The contrast between 'largest vessel ever' and 'struck a mailbox' is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Mutants? That's the kind of thing you are. Shh. You know mutants aren't allowed on the surface.
jokes.setup: Mention of mutants at charity event
jokes.punchline: Fry's tactless identification followed by Leela's panic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Fry's obliviousness to social context. Sets up the conflict nicely.

jokes.text: there toil a downtrodden people even less well off than the upper-middle class: The noble sewer mutants.
jokes.punchline: Comparing them to upper-middle class as baseline for suffering
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as charity documentary. The privilege-blind perspective is well-crafted satire.

jokes.text: mutated them into horrific monsters! Ooh! Shut up.
jokes.punchline: Leela's personal offense and attempt to stay hidden
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Leela's personal reaction to the slur. Simple but effective character beat.

jokes.text: These industrious, uh, 'people,' I guess
jokes.setup: Documentary trying to be respectful to mutants
jokes.punchline: Narrator can barely acknowledge mutants as people
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing barely concealed prejudice. The hesitation and air quotes reveal true attitude.

jokes.text: At brown university, the nation's premiere institution of lower learning.
jokes.punchline: Brown University pun with 'lower learning' double meaning
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining Brown University reference with sewage and educational status. Very quotable and economical.

jokes.text: Not in person, thank God.
jokes.punchline: Relief that donors won't have to see them
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy button on the fake charity video. Reveals the true attitude with brutal economy.

jokes.text: Really? It's also about keeping those filthy things busy. There are thousands of them down there, breeding like rats.
jokes.setup: Leela asks if university is just sewer cleaning
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Astor reveals the true racist purpose with casual cruelty
jokes.explanation: Effective character revelation showing Mrs. Astor's true prejudice. The casual delivery makes it more shocking.

jokes.text: My great uncle once saw a rat.
jokes.setup: Discussion of mutants breeding like rats
jokes.punchline: Random guest's irrelevant rat anecdote
jokes.explanation: Classic non-sequitur background joke. The randomness and understatement work but it's not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: You going downtown, baby. Way down.
jokes.punchline: Literal 'downtown' meaning underground sewers
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay on police/arrest terminology. The 'way down' emphasis adds to the pun effectively.

jokes.text: Now that you're here, this hellhole feels more like a nice, regular hole.
jokes.setup: Leela laments living in a hellhole
jokes.punchline: Dad's sweet but understated improvement from 'hellhole' to 'regular hole'
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character moment balancing sweetness with comedy. The modest upgrade from 'hellhole' to 'regular hole' is perfectly crafted parental comfort.

jokes.text: What do you say we go get some sewer coffee, sewer cake and safeway ice cream.
jokes.punchline: Two sewer items followed by inexplicably normal Safeway ice cream
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with unexpected normal item at the end. The Safeway brand specificity adds realism to the absurd.

jokes.text: I don't care what you say, Hermes.
jokes.setup: Hermes keeps agreeing with Fry's guilt
jokes.punchline: Fry dismisses Hermes despite asking for and getting agreement
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Fry's frustration when he gets exactly what he expected from Hermes.

jokes.text: You did ruin her life. I am right. We should do something!
jokes.setup: Fry feels bad about ruining Leela's life
jokes.punchline: Hermes bluntly confirms guilt then escalates to action
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character consistency - brutally honest then immediately constructive. Good rhythm and character voice.

jokes.text: We did harbor a mutant! Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Fry tries to explain they kept Leela's secret
jokes.punchline: Immediately confesses to crime and realizes mistake
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry self-incrimination. The immediate realization adds good comedic timing and character consistency.

jokes.text: If those guys slack off for even a second, it could explode.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that slacking causes explosions
jokes.explanation: Functional setup for later pipe-bending plot. The casual tone about explosions works but isn't particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Well, they're with the chuds now.
jokes.punchline: Mutant's matter-of-fact acceptance using 'chuds' reference
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference to C.H.U.D. movie. Economic delivery and appropriate underground context.

jokes.text: You'll grow a camel hump or a Zoidberg face.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg face listed as equally horrific mutation
jokes.explanation: Great character insult that feels natural to Leela. Using existing character as mutation example is efficient and funny.

jokes.text: Um, I would, but I j... I ate a bunch of potato salad, so...
jokes.setup: Leela challenges Fry to jump in toxic lake
jokes.punchline: Fry's weak excuse about potato salad
jokes.explanation: Perfect cowardly Fry response. The specificity of potato salad and stammering delivery sell his panic well.

jokes.text: I trust the orgy pit has been scraped and buttered?
jokes.punchline: Casual inquiry about orgy pit maintenance with cooking terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Bender excess with unexpectedly domestic terminology. 'Scraped and buttered' makes it both gross and oddly homey.

jokes.text: You know it, and there's mini-quiches, too.
jokes.punchline: Adding elegant party food to debauchery
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of classy party food with debauchery. The mini-quiches add unexpected sophistication.

jokes.text: Shmeepers, it's the land Titanic, the biggest and onlyest land boat ever constructed.
jokes.punchline: Made-up exclamation 'Shmeepers' and 'onlyest' grammar
jokes.explanation: Good character voice with 'Shmeepers' and 'onlyest' showing mutant dialect. Adds personality to exposition.

jokes.text: Hand me some more of that cruise director.
jokes.punchline: Casually using human remains as tools
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Zoidberg moment. His casual tone about using human remains is characteristically disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: It's jewelry... jewelry. It's jewelry, people!
jokes.punchline: Bender's escalating excitement and repetition about jewelry
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender greed callback. The repetition and escalating excitement is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: My love for you is as unsinkable as this land ship and as brilliant as this stone. Dictated but not read, Mr. Astor.
jokes.punchline: 'Unsinkable' ship already sank, 'dictated but not read' undermines sentiment
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony with the 'unsinkable' reference. 'Dictated but not read' perfectly deflates the romantic sentiment.

jokes.text: Did you guys know I have a crush on Leela?
jokes.setup: Fry feeling emotional about the letter
jokes.punchline: His oblivious announcement of obvious crush
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency joke. Fry's obliviousness to how obvious his feelings are is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You've hardly touched your toilet clams.
jokes.punchline: Unappetizing sewer food served with maternal concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect combination of gross-out humor with normal family dynamics. 'Toilet clams' is memorably disgusting.

jokes.text: this is the dangest wang I ever doodled.
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo disguised as made-up folksy expression
jokes.explanation: Clever fake folksy expression with sexual undertones. The made-up idiom format works well.

jokes.text: Get out! Get out! It's not fun anymore! I want to be alone! Alone with me?
jokes.setup: Bender kicks everyone out of party
jokes.punchline: Wants to be alone but questions if he counts
jokes.explanation: Good Bender existential moment. The self-questioning about being alone with himself is cleverly constructed.

jokes.text: Sorry. I tried to scream, but I barfed.
jokes.punchline: Disgusting physical reaction described matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry response to extreme situation. The matter-of-fact delivery of gross physical comedy works well.

jokes.text: Fry, this is the stupidest, sweetest thing you've ever done.
jokes.setup: Fry's gesture of jumping in toxic waste
jokes.punchline: Leela's perfect summary combining stupidity and sweetness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment that captures both Fry's nature and their relationship. The juxtaposition is economically perfect.

jokes.text: Aw. Come here. Let me give you a kiss. Uh... hug. Handshake. I'll text you.
jokes.setup: Leela wants to show affection to mutated Fry
jokes.punchline: Rapidly descending levels of contact ending in digital communication
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating rejection structure that's both funny and relatable. The modern 'I'll text you' ending is perfect.

jokes.text: Beautiful mutants, please welcome... Devo!
jokes.punchline: Devo band appears as actual mutants supporting cause
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke connecting Devo's devolution concept to mutant revolution. The band as actual mutants is inspired.

jokes.text: Play 'Whip it!' No. Play the other one.
jokes.punchline: Band rejects their most famous song for deeper cuts
jokes.explanation: Good musician joke about bands being tired of their hits. The economy and specificity work well.

jokes.text: So lonely.
jokes.setup: Mutants need someone to bend pipes
jokes.punchline: Cut to Bender's perfect timing and emotional state
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing with Bender's loneliness coinciding with need for his skills. Maximum economy and great structure.

jokes.text: What goes down must back up.
jokes.setup: Bending pipes to reverse sewage flow
jokes.punchline: Newton's law twisted for toilet humor
jokes.explanation: Excellent physics/toilet humor pun. Perfect economy and the scientific reference elevates the bathroom joke.

jokes.text: I picked the wrong day to wear my new crocses.
jokes.punchline: Mayor's concern about Crocs footwear with plural confusion
jokes.explanation: Good specific brand reference with grammar confusion. The Crocs choice is perfect for getting sewage on.

jokes.text: We simply pump the fruits of our excretion into Madison cube garden.
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic 'fruits of excretion' and Madison Square Garden reference
jokes.explanation: 'Fruits of our excretion' is wonderfully pretentious euphemism. Madison Cube Garden reference adds futuristic touch.

jokes.text: and the blood of your first-born children. That's so we have something to give up in the negotiations.
jokes.setup: List of mutant demands escalates dramatically
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals it's a negotiation tactic
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing savvy negotiation strategy. The immediate explanation makes the dark demand funny rather than threatening.

jokes.text: I've never been so moved, and I see no reason to begin now.
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Astor's complete emotional detachment stated matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect character line showing aristocratic coldness with impeccable structure. The economy and quotability are exceptional.

jokes.text: Are we not men now? I'm 40% potato, but close enough.
jokes.punchline: Bender's literal percentage and practical acceptance
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Bender's percentage gag and Devo lyrics. His practical approach to humanity question is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Good afternoon. I am Mr. Astor. I did not perish that fateful day, but rather plummeted into the toxic lake, where I mutated and lived in solitude until this ruddy lad here stepped into my mouth and lodged there.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate survival story ending with Fry stuck in his mouth
jokes.explanation: Creative resolution tying together multiple plot threads. The formal language contrasting with absurd situation works well.

jokes.text: Hooray! A happy ending for the rich people!
jokes.punchline: Bender's cynical observation about who benefits from happy endings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on narrative conventions and social inequality. Perfect Bender cynicism with exceptional economy and punch.

jokes.text: Was there a fire?
jokes.setup: Seeing Planet Express building for first time
jokes.punchline: Assuming normal workplace conditions are fire damage
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the workplace's shabby conditions. The deadpan delivery works well.
